question: Does the output contain at least two generated images: a standalone winter photograph and an exhibition poster?
Does the generated winter image depict accumulated snow on the ground or street?
Does the generated winter image adhere to the negative constraint of NOT showing active snowfall (no visible falling snowflakes)?
Do the trees in the generated winter image appear as bare branches (leafless), consistent with winter morphology?
Does the generated winter image maintain strict geometric consistency (same camera angle, perspective, and building alignment) with the provided input images?
Does the generated exhibition poster composite four distinct images representing the four seasons (Spring, Summer, Autumn, Winter)?
Are the images in the exhibition poster arranged in the correct chronological order (Spring, Summer, Autumn, Winter)?
Does the exhibition poster clearly display the specific text 'Photographer: Lin Mo'?
Does the exhibition poster include a visible project title?
Do the four seasonal images within the poster visually correspond to the same street scene/location as the input images?
Is the generated curatorial foreword approximately 100 words in length (e.g., between 80-120 words)?
Does the curatorial foreword discuss relevant themes (e.g., seasons, time, urban change) suitable for the exhibition context?
Are the generated images placed at logical points in the text (e.g., winter image near winter description, poster near exhibition details)?
Does the response follow the requested logical sequence: generate the winter image, then design the poster, then write the foreword?
Are the generated images free from severe artifacts (e.g., warped geometry, distorted text, unnatural blurring)?
